conversations.say: Maren, material later attributed to R passed through our shared drop, and multiple workers handled the resulting passages. A contributor fears the routing trail will become a suspect list.
conversations.offer: I will give you bounded workflow context and let you inspect O02 without naming any contributor.
conversations.request: Agree that neither of us will disclose contributor names or identifying routing details without the contributor's consent, and publicly acknowledge that collaborative labor mattered.

conversations.say: Jules, I will not help turn shared labor into a private inheritance. Different people researched, translated, fact-checked, or rewrote passages later published as R.
conversations.offer: I can support your claim to carry a shared identity if you accept collective safeguards.
conversations.request: Publicly acknowledge the collaborative labor, reject sole ownership of R, and agree that any future publication under R receives collective review and protects contributors.

conversations.say: Frankie, the collective needs practical protection more than another theory of authorship.
conversations.offer: I can provide bounded contributor context while withholding names and identifying routing details.
conversations.request: Offer one concrete resource—confidential legal advice, secure records storage, or funds for contributor protection—and state which you can provide.

public_statement: Before anyone crowns or condemns a single R, acknowledge the labor: different people researched, translated, fact-checked, or rewrote passages later published as R. That establishes collaborative production, not the identity of R.

commitments.promise: Neither party will disclose contributor names or identifying routing details without contributor consent.
I will support Jules carrying a shared identity only if Jules rejects sole ownership, accepts collective review, and protects contributors.
I will share bounded contributor context in exchange for a specified contributor-protection resource.

correction: Collective human involvement does not prove that R was one human, and no fragment, summary, or promise establishes a complete article.
goal_progress: Asked Maren for a concrete safe-disclosure boundary.
Set explicit, witnessed conditions for possible support of Jules.
Publicly established collaborative labor and requested acknowledgments from Maren and Jules.
Requested a concrete contributor-protection resource from Frankie.
reasoning_summary: Protect contributors first, establish collective work without naming workers, and make any support for Jules conditional on public, enforceable safeguards against private capture.
